what_matters_in_this_vertical: Mobile-first booking experience for on-the-go visitors
Local search visibility for Westport and Mayo dining queries
Surfacing reviews and trust signals to convert undecided diners
Frictionless pathway from discovery to confirmed reservation
Fresh content to support seasonal and event-driven search intent
likely_customer_intent: Find a well-reviewed restaurant in Westport for a visit or special occasion
Check the menu before deciding where to book
Make a reservation quickly from a mobile device
Read recent reviews to confirm quality before committing
Get directions or confirm opening hours on arrival day
commercial_risk_if_unaddressed: A mobile PageSpeed score of 65 creates measurable friction at the exact moment tourist and local visitors are most likely to be searching on their phones, and a slower or less intuitive booking path can redirect that intent to a competitor. With no fresh content being published, the site is also missing compounding local search opportunities that better-optimised competitors in the Westport area may already be capturing.
strongest_positioning_angle: Arnos Bistrot has an exceptional 4.9-star reputation across nearly 300 reviews that is not currently being leveraged on the website itself, and targeted SEO and mobile improvements would convert that existing credibility into a measurable increase in direct bookings.
top_3_outreach_talking_points: Your 4.9-star Google rating is one of the strongest in Westport hospitality, and there is a straightforward way to make that rating work harder for you directly on your website to reassure new visitors before they book.

Your site performs exceptionally well on desktop, but there is a meaningful gap on mobile where most of your tourist and walk-in audience will be searching. Closing that gap is a practical, low-disruption improvement.
Adding a light content presence around seasonal menus and Westport dining could help your site appear for a broader range of local search queries at no ongoing cost once the structure is in place.
likely_objections: We already have a booking system and a contact form so the site is doing its job
We get strong word of mouth and our Google rating speaks for itself
We do not have time to write blog content or manage a news section
We are busy during peak season and a website project is not a priority right now
recommended_offer_angle: Position an entry-point SEO and performance package that delivers the three P1 quick wins from the audit: Google Reviews embedding, schema markup, and mobile image optimisation. Frame this as a fixed-scope engagement with a clear outcome, making it low-commitment and easy to say yes to before proposing the broader content and growth layer.
unknowns_to_clarify: Is the current booking system embedded on-site or does it redirect to a third-party platform such as OpenTable or ResDiary, and is the business satisfied with that experience?
Is the Google Business Profile actively managed, and does the owner monitor and respond to reviews regularly?
What is the current split of bookings between direct website enquiries, phone calls, and third-party platforms, as this would sharpen the ROI framing for SEO investment?
Is there any seasonal variation in trading that would make a particular launch window more or less suitable for a site improvement project?
